title: Pilosocereus millspaughii
intro: Pilosocereus millspaughii, also known as the 'Candelabra Cactus', is a species of cactus that belongs to the genus Pilosocereus, characterized by its tall, columnar stems covered with sharp spines. The health benefits of Pilosocereus millspaughii include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pilosocereus millspaughii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.

This species belongs to the genus Pilosocereus, which is a group of cacti characterized by their cylindrical stems and areoles that produce dense, fine, and long spines. The species is native to the Americas and is found in countries such as Mexico and Costa Rica.
